source.notes: Optimus _ EFL  - 2026_05_06 12_59 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: This was an intro/demo call between Optimus (founder/CEO Ed Stockman and Max Riemer, who drove the demo) and EFL, a global forwarder trying to grow a smaller domestic freight product. Optimus pitched a sales-lead engine built on proprietary factoring-company invoice/POD data — wallet-share expansion, lookalike new logos, and two-way backhaul matching — and ran a live demo on EFL's own lanes (Chicago↔Columbus, Niagara Bottling, Costco trading partners). The call ended warm: Ed committed to a same-day warm intro to Niagara's procurement head and a 12-month deal with TMS integration in 90 days, but the firm next step with decision-owner Maggie (who left early) was left open.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Ed consistently adapted to what buyers actually raised — running a live Smith Optics side-by-side the moment Cooper flagged Revenue Vessel's bad emails, and re-checking NEIL's exact backhaul direction before searching — and his arousal built across the call, even though he ran flatter than his own baseline (valence z -1.5, arousal z -1.2), which weighs less than his clear transcript responsiveness.

evidence.quote: Max, will you go into Smith Optics? I want to do a side by side right now.
Wait. So, you want to do Chicago to Columbus or Columbus to Chicago?

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could summarize it: Optimus uses proprietary factoring-company invoice/POD data to tell brokers who to call, why, and when, expanding wallet share and finding new logos with a per-dollar ROI commitment.

evidence.quote: being able to tell um uh brokers and forwarders who to call, why to call them, what's the value prop, what should you be saying to that shipper, and when to call them
for every dollar you spend, you get X amount back from Optimus

relevance.explanation: The pitch was tailored to EFL's actual network — their ocean-transload flows into Groveport, their requested Chicago-to-Columbus backhaul, and Niagara Bottling's real top lanes — and spoke directly to NEIL's procurement role.

evidence.quote: If you're if you guys are moving a lot of the import goods into Groveport, then a lot of their their domestic is going to be coming out of that facility.
if you go into Niagara Bottling, scroll down, there are two top lanes, Gahana, Ohio to Chicago and Gahana to Wanetka, Illinois

discovery_progression.explanation: Ed asked productive qualifying questions (entity structure, TMS, sales headcount) and landed concrete dated commitments — a same-day Niagara warm intro and a 90-day TMS integration — though the firm next meeting with decision-owner Maggie was never dated.

evidence.quote: Um is everything done on the same TMS?
I will text him uh in 30 minutes after my next call and try to just tee up a warm

Ed Stockman.coaching_action: Before ending a warm call, lock a specific dated next step with the true decision-maker — here, book Maggie for a follow-up on a set date — instead of leaving momentum to your own action items.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max drove the demo responsively to NEIL's live requests, inverting the lane on cue and surfacing the facility filter for backhauls, with healthy pitch variation (8.93 st) and above-baseline valence.

evidence.quote: if you have a facility somewhere where you're looking for back holes, you can use the facility filter

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Max's spoken contributions centered on demo mechanics and onboarding logistics (HubSpot integration, cohorts, sending emails) rather than the quantified 'why care'; the ROI narrative came entirely from Ed, so a neutral listener couldn't derive the value prop from Max's words alone.

evidence.quote: I know y'all are in HubSpot. We do have a native HubSpot integration.
it's very just get everyone in there and start

relevance.explanation: Max tied the demo to EFL specifically — inverting their real Chicago-Columbus lane, showing the backhaul facility filter, and noting their existing HubSpot stack for a native integration.

evidence.quote: if you have a facility somewhere where you're looking for back holes, you can use the facility filter
I know y'all are in HubSpot. We do have a native HubSpot integration.

discovery_progression.explanation: Max's questions were rapport- and navigation-level, and while he explained onboarding cohorts, he didn't probe EFL's needs or own a dated next step, deferring progression to Ed.

evidence.quote: Where are you uh calling from?
we have a we do so we do have an onboarding date. Do monthly cohorts.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: As you drive the demo, translate each screen into EFL's ROI in your own words (e.g., 'these 157 backhaul matches could cut your one-way Chicago–Columbus cost'), instead of narrating features and leaving the value story to Ed.

signals.signal: Own-words value recognition — visualized using it immediately
signals.quote: soon as you showed it, my mind started racing on how I would leverage the system

signals.signal: Built the use case himself with his own volume numbers
signals.quote: we have close to a hundred full truckloads coming into the Columbus market every week from Chicago. Then we could work together to present to create some interesting pricing

signals.signal: Buyer-owned next step — volunteered to pursue the Niagara intro
signals.quote: I'd be more than happy to uh follow up on that one um if you were to share that information.

signals.signal: Qualifier — routed the actual decision to Maggie
signals.quote: this is Maggie who's going to be running this down
already I could definitely see the the contacts seem to be better than Revenue Vessel.
how long would be like an onboarding time if we like went with you guys to like like start getting everything going?

signals.signal: Concrete incumbent pain motivating a switch
signals.quote: four out of the five emails they pulled like personal emails

signals.signal: Endorsed usability but owns no next step (not the decision-maker)
signals.quote: it seems pretty pretty straightforward and user friendly. So, I mean, I I can't imagine anyone would have too much trouble

signals.signal: Stated the specific gap driving interest
signals.quote: giving us the domestic data so much and that's why we're interested in you
Let's see it. Let's see it.

signals.signal: Informed and dissatisfied with the incumbent (Revenue Vessel pricing)
signals.quote: they're um because we're one of their customers and we're probably too close with them. Um and they have now increased their price quite a lot.

signals.signal: Low engagement — left the call early despite owning the decision
